# Generated Files Specification

Generated files are the main interface between the CLI, humans, and AI coding agents.

Source of truth: `OUTPUT_FILES` in `src/types.ts`.

---

## 1. Shared Rules

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Encoding | UTF-8 |
| Line endings | `\n` |
| Determinism | Same project state should generate the same output |
| Safe writes | Existing files are preserved unless `--force` is explicit |

### Generated Marker

Markdown files use an HTML comment marker:

```md
<!-- ready-for-agents:generated file="<output-file>" hash="<sha256-prefix>" -->
```

YAML files use a comment marker:

```yaml
# ready-for-agents:generated file="<output-file>" hash="<sha256-prefix>"
```

The hash is computed over the generated body after removing the marker. `update` and `diff` use this marker to distinguish generated files from user-authored files.

---

## 2. `AGENTS.md`

Purpose: instructions for AI coding agents working in the target repository.

Required sections:

| Section | Contents |
| --- | --- |
| Project Goal | project name and stack summary |
| How To Work In This Repo | package manager, stack, folders |
| Agent Context Workflow | tree/query-first workflow |
| Important Rules | safe behavior and local conventions |
| Files To Avoid Editing | lockfiles, generated output, context cache |
| Testing Expectations | test/build fallback guidance |

---

## 3. `PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`

Purpose: technical snapshot of the target project.

Required sections:

| Section | Contents |
| --- | --- |
| Project Name | `package.json` name |
| Stack | frontend, backend, database, and dependency evidence |
| Package Manager | detected manager and source |
| Framework | framework summary |
| Database | database summary |
| Important Folders | detected root folders |
| Agent Context Index | role of generated context files and tree |
| Dependencies | dependency and devDependency summaries |
| Notes | README/fallback/backend/generated notes |

---

## 4. `COMMANDS.md`

Purpose: copy-paste commands for setup, development, verification, and agent context lookup.

Required sections:

| Section | Contents |
| --- | --- |
| Setup | install command by package manager |
| Development | main dev script, if detected |
| Related Development Scripts | related `dev:*` scripts, if detected |
| Build | build script, if detected |
| Test | test script, if detected |
| Lint | lint script, if detected |
| Typecheck | typecheck script, if detected |
| Format | format script, if detected |
| Agent Context | `rfa query` and `rfa index` workflow |

---

## 5. `RUNBOOK.md`

Command: `runbook`.

Purpose: privacy-safe guide for reviving and running an old project.

Required sections:

| Section | Contents |
| --- | --- |
| Project Summary | stack, database, package manager, folders |
| Privacy And Data Safety | explicit secret handling rules |
| Environment | variable names and sources, never values |
| Setup | install command |
| Development | dev script |
| Related Development Scripts | related dev scripts |
| Build/Test/Lint/Typecheck | verification commands |
| Runtime Notes | frontend/backend/database notes |
| Revival Checklist | operational startup sequence |
| Agent Handoff | suggested `doctor` and `query` workflow |

Privacy rules:

- Do not read `.env*` non-template values.
- Parse only variable names from safe templates.
- Extract only static env names from source code.
- Never emit secret values.

---

## 6. Optional Agent-Native Files

| File | Generated By | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `.cursor/rules/ready-for-agents.mdc` | `init --cursor`, `--all`, update/fix presets | Cursor project rule |
| `CLAUDE.md` | `init --claude`, `--all`, update/fix presets | Claude Code guidance |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | `init --copilot`, `--all`, update/fix presets | GitHub Copilot repository instructions |

---

## 7. GitHub Actions Workflow

File: `.github/workflows/ready-for-agents.yml`  
Command: `ci`

The workflow runs `rfa doctor --json` and `rfa diff --json` on push and pull request events.

---

## 8. Context Tree Cache

File: `.ready-for-agents/context-tree.json`  
Command: `index`, or generated by `init` / `update` / `doctor --fix` when index is enabled.

Purpose: compact machine-readable map of generated file sections.

The JSON cache does not use a generated marker because it is not Markdown/YAML and can always be regenerated.

---

## 9. Config File

File: `.ready-for-agents.json`  
Command: `config init`

The config file is intentionally not part of `OUTPUT_FILES` and does not use a generated marker.
